message:
feat(bytes): add read extensions, write extensions, chain reader, and take reader to io_adapter

Extend the async I/O adapter module with higher-level convenience
functions and combinator-style reader adapters, all gated behind the
same ASX_HAS_BROWSER_IO compile-time guard as the rest of the adapter
family.

Read extensions:
- asx_io_read_exact(): reads exactly n bytes from a read adapter into
  a mutable buffer, looping over poll_read calls until the requested
  count is satisfied. Returns ASX_E_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL if the source
  hits EOF before n bytes, ASX_E_WOULD_BLOCK on pending, and
  ASX_E_INVALID_STATE on read error. Always reports partial progress
  via the bytes_read out-parameter.
- asx_io_read_to_end(): drains a read adapter until EOF, accumulating
  all bytes into the destination buffer. Reports total bytes read and
  uses the same error mapping as read_exact for pending/error states.

Write extensions:
- asx_io_write_all(): writes the entirety of an asx_buf through a
  write adapter, looping over poll_write until all bytes are consumed.
  Returns ASX_E_DISCONNECTED if the sink closes, ASX_E_WOULD_BLOCK on
  pending, and ASX_E_INVALID_STATE on write error.

Chain reader (asx_chain_reader):
- Concatenates two read adapters sequentially: reads from the first
  adapter until EOF, then transparently continues from the second.
- asx_chain_reader_init() stores both adapters and the done-flag.
- asx_chain_reader_adapter() returns a new asx_read_adapter whose
  poll_read dispatches through the chain state machine.

Take reader (asx_take_reader):
- Limits reads from an inner adapter to at most N bytes total.
- Tracks remaining byte budget and limits the destination buffer
  capacity on each poll_read call. Returns ASX_READ_EOF once the
  budget is exhausted, even if the inner adapter has more data.
- asx_take_reader_init() stores the inner adapter and byte limit.
- asx_take_reader_adapter() returns a wrapped asx_read_adapter.

All new functions perform NULL-argument validation and return
ASX_E_INVALID_ARGUMENT for NULL inputs where applicable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
